content:
Hello there Codywholers.

Extually it does regonise it if all of it is going wrong. In Linux you can change settings for that auto updates in thunderbird also.

That might explain the message. If it was overlooked, then there should be more issues surrounding that topic.

How to turn off or on these settings?

Debian 1. Disable the unattended-upgrades service: 2. Disable systemd timers 3. Edit the auto-upgrade configuration file Open the file using sudo nano /etc/apt/apt.conf.d/20auto-upgrades and change both values to "0" so it looks exactly like this:

text : apt::periodic::update-package-lists “0”; apt::periodic::unattended-upgrade “0”;

In thunderbird Menu icon>select settings>general tab , scroll down to>updates >change the settings to check for updates but let the user choose wether to install them.

Other types of system software can use other types of configuration.

Use https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/channel-choices-thunderbird-snap-and-flatpak

Tip Thunderbird doesn't have permission to modify files outside its container. Instead, Flatpak and your Linux system handle these tasks.

content: That is a Surfshark question. Most VPNs have settings to bypass checking of email, so my guess is that Surfshark does too. Thunderbird has no feature that lets it ignore VPNs, as that would defeat the purpose of a VPN.
creator: davidsk

content:
1. Ich bitte, die Antwort zu prüfen: Bei Versand in Thunderbird kommt, wenn ich Surfshark-VPN geschaltet habe, die Nachricht: "Senden der Nachricht fehlgeschlagen. Fehler beim Senden der Nachricht: SMTP-Server-Fehler. Der Mail-Server antwortete: IP=149.88.19.87 - Your address is blocked." Für Surfshark ist diese Rückmeldung ein Hinweis, dass die IP-Adresse beim Senden der E-Mails gesperrt sein könnte.

2. Ich habe bei Thunderbird immer wieder Anmeldeprobleme gehabt. Nur der Port 465 funktioniert im Postausgangsserver. Der ebenfalls in Frage kommende Port 2525 funktioniert beim Versand nicht.

Ich freue mich über jeden Hinweis, der die volle Funktion ermöglicht. Jörg
creator: j-schwenk

content: Disable Surfshark first and ensure that thunderbird is working properly. Thunderbird has no defense against any VPN, and they prevent email to server connections. Once thunderbird sends and receives normally, then tweak the VPN to stay out of the way. As an aside, your issue is not new; we regularly see users having troubles with VPNs.

content:
Hello there



Hi everyone!

I'm trying to log into my email account on the domain example.com via SMTP. The email account is with yandex.ru. When I try to authenticate, it says the SMTP password is incorrect. I decided to try through Outlook, and it worked. What could be the issue? 

The Problem:The email client is failing to authenticate or connect with the server. 
It appears that Thunderbird is confused by cached credentials from a previously used Google environment instead of connecting to Yandex.2.

Proposed Solution (Step-by-Step):Clear Cached Passwords: Open Thunderbird, go to your saved passwords menu (Settings > Privacy & Security > Saved Passwords), and clear all old Yandex and Google data.(refresh).

Remove Old Data & Cache: Clear Thunderbird's cache to ensure absolutely no legacy Google or Yandex credentials are being referenced.Set Up OAuth 2.0: Re-add your Yandex account to Thunderbird. Ensure your incoming and outgoing (SMTP) servers are configured to use OAuth2 as the authentication method.

Generate App Password (If needed): If OAuth2 fails or gives an error, log into your Yandex webmail, generate an "App Password" (or Token), and use "Normal Password" as the authentication method in Thunderbird with that token.

Look at the domain names that they are not directed to earlier google data.

Check the domain names that were not redirected to the earlier Google data. After encountering either an OAuth 2.0 protocol issue or a lack of security, attempting to configure OAuth 2.0 resulted in a denial and redirection to Google. Change this behaviour to perform a complete refresh.(deleting all caches , that likely contains this earlier data sets)

Tip Profile Sanitization: Inspect and clean the configuration registry (prefs.js) to ensure no residual SMTP/IMAP server mappings from the previous environment are active.


Tip2

Cache Purge: Clear Thunderbird’s internal password manager, cookies, and network cache to remove all legacy OAuth2 artifacts.

Profile Sanitization: Inspect and clean the configuration registry (prefs.js) to ensure no residual SMTP/IMAP server mappings from the previous environment are active.

Protocol Standardization: Re-configure the Yandex account using strict OAuth2 parameters (or Yandex App Passwords, depending on current server-side policy) on a clean authentication pipeline.
creator: Googlethunderbird.

content:
Have you used the Search function to see if they are found?

I'm guessing that you are displaying the message list in Threaded mode. This makes all messages involved in a "conversation" be grouped together, distinguishable by a little "tree" icon. The group can be collapsed, which can hide some of the responses from view. Here's an article about the feature and how to enable and disable it.

https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/message-threading-thunderbird

Personally, the feature isn't very useful to me, so I have it turned off. I prefer to see the list in strictly date order, and if I want to find a conversation I'll use Quick Search.

Is it the "From" information that is not to your liking? Are you talking about when viewing the message itself or its listing in the Message List, or both? If the list only and the column name is "Correspondents", try adding a "From" column, which can show slightly different information.

Another setting you can try is found at <Settings | General | Message List>. It has three "Preferred address display format" settings. One of them might be more to your liking.

The actual "From' field is created by the "third-party application" and you can't change that.

Another though occurs to me - if there are a finite number of customers, you could create a Tag for each one, then create a Message Filter that would color the messages from each or move them into their own folder.

content: In mbox files, a deleted message remains, but is marked for delete. That means that it still is taking up space on your computer but is not accessible. As deleted messages increase, this increases the size of the folder. The process of compacting is removing the 'dead' messages from the folder. This is just basic disc space maintenance.

content: Here is a brief, accurate response you can use for the Thunderbird forum: By default, Thunderbird sorts folders in a specific hierarchy (like Inbox, Drafts, Sent, Trash first) and then alphabetically for custom folders. It does not automatically sort them by the "creation date." If your custom folders are not sorting alphabetically, or if you want to order them exactly how you like, here are the solutions: 1. Manual Sorting (Thunderbird 102 and newer) You can easily drag and drop folders into any order you prefer: Click and hold the folder you want to move. Drag it up or down to your preferred position and release. 2. Force Alphabetical Sorting If your custom folders are out of order and you want to force them back into alphabetical order: Right-click on your account name (the main email folder at the top of the list). Select Settings. (Optional) For advanced control over folder behavior, you can look into the Advanced Preferences (about:config) and search for mail.account.accountX.folder_order, but dragging and dropping is the recommended way in modern versions. 3. Use an Add-on for Advanced Sorting If you have a very large number of folders and want automated, strict sorting rules, you can install a community extension: Go to Settings > Extensions & Themes. Search for and install Manually Sort Folders. This add-on gives you full control over account and folder ordering if the built-in drag-and-drop isn't enough.

content:
I assume the dup is https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/questions/1583944

Not to be honest I hav4e no idea what "How can I get just the normal readable text quoted (Gmail-style)?" means to you and I am assuming because I use a gmail account, not the gmail interface. I did not like the way they hid quoted text back when and have avoided that ever since using a mailing client instead. What I can say is the Google way is not always the right way usually. They have their own ideas involving selling adds that take precedence as far as they are concerned.

I would like to see more of this than the mangled text this website offers. I think what you are seeing is the raw HTML behind the message. Now if it was for only a specific email, it may well be the poor way it is put together bu the sender. Unfortunately commercial email is a complex issue and the space is full of folk that think if they bulk email from word it will work. The result is you often have trouble finding folk that will engage with formatting issues.

If it is all emails there are a couple of experiments to try to see if you can work out what the cause is rather than concentrate on the specific of an individual sender.

When you click forward do you have the formatting bar above the body of the text. Sometimes things cause Thunderbird to try and use plain text and the conversion of HTML is messy the formatting toolbar is not present in plain text mode (holding shift when you click "+New MEssage" forces a one off toggle between modes).

So is the formatting bar present?

The next thing is does this occur in troubleshooting mode? These sorts of issues are unfortunately often caused by unauthorized addons injected into Thunderbird by security software as well as being the side effect of other addons that did not make a transition of versions well. So trying troubleshooting mode will point, or not, to another possible cause (addons and themes).

As David intimated right at the beginning the header setting can make a forward or reply messy, but that is only in quoting the message headers, not mangling the HTML used in the message. SO I would discount that as a possibility as this does appear to involve a hyperlink.

Additionally is 5the entire massage text a mess of markup, or is it just the link or links?
creator: MattAuSupport

content:
Solved it! Pressing shift while hitting Reply solved the problem and yes, it was the entire message that was a mess of markup. I had tried troubleshooting mode to no avail.

Thanks much, Matt!
